introduction.value: Located on the River Clyde in West Central Scotland, Glasgow is famed for its bold architecture, vibrant art scene, world-class museums, and top-tier music venues. The city also boasts an incredible food and drink scene, thriving nightlife, and excellent shopping. Recently named the UK’s friendliest city by Condé Nast Traveller, it’s one of the most exciting and welcoming cities you’ll ever visit.

copy.value:
Glasgow 2026 Festival, 23 May – 9 August 2026 – outdoor performances, community celebrations, innovative arts, music and more will make summer 2026 a truly unmissable experience.

Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games, 23 July – 2 August 2026 – mark your calendar for 11 days of world-class action across the city, bringing 3,000 athletes from 74 nations together to compete across 10 sports.

Game On Live Site, 23 July – 2 August 2026 – get into the excitement of Glasgow 2026 at Kelvingrove Park, with free sports activities, pop-up games areas and live Commonwealth Games action on the big screen as part of the Glasgow 2026 Festival.

SWG3 live music events, 24 July – 1 August 2026 – enjoy five nights of live music at SWG3 as part of the Glasgow 2026 Festival, featuring Kyle Falconer, Mungo's HiFi, Hot Chip (DJ set) and celtic folk legends Capercaillie alongside acclaimed contemporary trad band Rura.

Merchant City Festival, 24 July - 2 August 2026 – enjoy arts, circus, dance, live music in the Merchant City area of Glasgow.

World Pipe Band Championships, 14 – 15 August 2026 – hear over 8,000 of the world's best pipers and drummers compete for the top prize in the largest event of its kind in the world.

Glasgow Foodies Festival, 14 – 16 August 2026 – featuring top chefs, tasty street food, craft drinks and live music in a relaxed outdoor setting.

properties.title: The Burrell Collection
properties.description: Visit The Burrell Collection in the heart of Pollok Country Park. This recently refurbished building is home to one of the greatest art collections with thousands of objects spread across 24 galleries and spanning more than 6,000 years of history. You'll find famous artworks from Rodin, Degas and Cézanne, as well as medieval, Chinese and Islamic art.

properties.title: Gallery of Modern Art
properties.description: GoMA is Scotland's most visited art gallery and the centre for Glasgow's extensive modern and contemporary art collection. ​​​​​​Found in the heart of Glasgow in Royal Exchange Square, with the famous Duke of Wellington Statue guarding out front (usually with a traffic cone atop his head), GoMA is free to enter.

properties.title: Glasgow Botanic Gardens
properties.description: Explore the pathways of Glasgow Botanic Gardens to see their collection of flora and shrubs before visiting Kibble Palace, an exquisite restored Victorian glasshouse. If you're feeling peckish, the tearoom offers a tasty selection of cakes, sandwiches, teas and coffees.

properties.title: Glasgow Cathedral
properties.description:
Built in the 1100s, Glasgow Cathedral is one of the oldest buildings in the city and one of the most intact medieval cathedrals on the Scottish mainland.

Visit to see its beautiful Gothic architecture and step down into the historic crypt, which was built in the mid-1200s to house the tomb of St Kentigern.

In Outlander the cathedral’s crypt doubles as L'Hopital Des Anges in Paris, where Claire volunteers to work.

properties.title: Glasgow City Chambers
properties.description: Completed in 1888 and overlooking George Square, Glasgow City Chambers is one of the city’s most prestigious buildings.

properties.title: Glasgow Science Centre
properties.description: Glasgow Science Centre is a great family attraction that presents science and technology in unique and inspiring ways.

properties.title: House for an Art Lover
properties.description: Just south of the River Clyde, explore the works of one of the city's favourite sons, Charles Rennie Mackintosh. The beautiful House for an Art Lover is a stunning Mackintosh design. Although it wasn't actually built in his lifetime, it's a true art nouveau dream fully realised, with the building and furnishings working in complete harmony.

link.label: Discover House for an Art Lover

properties.title: Hunterian Museum
properties.description:
Located in the historic University of Glasgow buildings, the Hunterian Museum has lots of fascinating exhibits and stories for you to uncover, including medicine, zoology and anatomy.

Make sure to visit Mackintosh House too, to see the home that Charles Rennie Mackintosh designed with his wife Margaret Macdonald Mackintosh.

Open: all year

properties.title: Kelvingrove Art Gallery & Museum
properties.description: Glasgow's trendy West End is home to Kelvingrove, a free museum packed with over 8,000 intriguing objects and artefacts. The museum houses an extraordinary collection of art, from the Dutch Old Masters and the French Impressionists, to perhaps its most famous piece, Christ of St John of the Cross by Salvador Dali.

properties.title: Riverside Museum and Tall Ship
properties.description: The Riverside Museum is a visual feast - the building itself is one of the most striking modern buildings in Scotland. Inside, the museum houses all kinds of exhibits related to transport. Vehicles, bikes, and locomotives dating from different periods can be found. In addition, you can also explore the Clyde-built Tall Ship, which is berthed alongside the museum.

link.label: Discover Riverside Museum and Tall Ship

copy.value: The people of Glasgow are ready to give you a warm and friendly welcome. Find out why the city is such a cultural hotspot.

Glasgow 2026 Festival, 23 May – 9 August 2026 – outdoor performances, community celebrations, innovative arts, music and more will make summer 2026 a truly unmissable experience.

Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games, 23 July – 2 August 2026 – mark your calendar for 11 days of world-class action across the city, bringing 3,000 athletes from 74 nations together to compete across 10 sports.

Game On Live Site, 23 July – 2 August 2026 – get into the excitement of Glasgow 2026 at Kelvingrove Park, with free sports activities, pop-up games areas and live Commonwealth Games action on the big screen as part of the Glasgow 2026 Festival.

SWG3 live music events, 24 July – 1 August 2026 – enjoy five nights of live music at SWG3 as part of the Glasgow 2026 Festival, featuring Kyle Falconer, Mungo's HiFi, Hot Chip (DJ set) and celtic folk legends Capercaillie alongside acclaimed contemporary trad band Rura.

Merchant City Festival, 24 July - 2 August 2026 – enjoy arts, circus, dance, live music in the Merchant City area of Glasgow.

World Pipe Band Championships, 14 – 15 August 2026 – hear over 8,000 of the world's best pipers and drummers compete for the top prize in the largest event of its kind in the world.

Glasgow Foodies Festival, 14 – 16 August 2026 – featuring top chefs, tasty street food, craft drinks and live music in a relaxed outdoor setting.

introduction.value: Rendez-vous dans la plus grande ville d’Écosse pour y découvrir des attractions de classe mondiale et l’une des meilleures destinations de shopping du Royaume-Uni. Vous pouvez aussi suivre le fleuve Clyde vers l’ouest via Paisley jusqu’à Greenock sur la côte, ou vers le sud en traversant les charmantes villes de la vallée de la Clyde, comme Lanark. En chemin, vous trouverez une campagne luxuriante, des destinations de sorties en famille, des sites classés au patrimoine mondial de l’UNESCO et une multitude d’histoires fascinantes.

introduction.value:
Avec son histoire riche, ses attractions de qualité et son architecture si caractéristique, Édimbourg est une ville que vous n’oublierez pas de sitôt. Explorez sa vieille ville médiévale et son élégante nouvelle ville de style georgien, et visitez des attractions uniques tout au long de votre séjour à Édimbourg. Venez découvrir la capitale écossaise, considérées comme l’une des plus belles villes du monde selon le magazine Time Out.

Veuillez noter qu’à compter du 1er octobre 2025, une taxe de séjour de 5 % sera appliquée aux nuits réservées dans les hébergements payants pour les réservations effectives à partir du 24 juillet 2026. Pour plus d’informations, rendez-vous sur la page du site ForeverEdinburgh.

Les courts séjours et les week-ends en Écosse peuvent vous procurer des plaisirs infinis qui vous donneront la sensation d’une véritable évasion. Laissez-vous inspirer par l’effervescence de nos villes ou planifiez une retraite de bien-être pour vous ressourcer dans les paysages naturels de l’Écosse. Les possibilités sont infinies !

introduction.value: Échappez au quotidien en partant à la découverte de la campagne écossaise. Nous avons la chance d’avoir des campagnes vallonnées à perte de vue, dont l’histoire et le travail de la terre remontent à plusieurs siècles. Le climat écossais se traduit par une abondance de verdure qui favorise la culture et l’élevage de produits locaux exceptionnels. Et le plus beau, c’est qu’il n’a jamais été aussi facile d’y accéder depuis les villes d’Écosse et d’Europe. Laissez l’agitation derrière vous, respirez l’air frais et vivifiant, observez les changements de saison et laissez-vous imprégner par notre nature au cours d’un séjour à la campagne.

Edinburgh ist die Hauptstadt von Schottland. Es ist eine Stadt mit viel Geschichte, Kultur, spannenden Sehenswürdigkeiten und wunderschönen Bauwerken. Erkunden Sie die gepflasterten Straßen und versteckten Gassen, besuchen Sie erstklassige Festivals und kosten Sie die fantastische Gastronomie in vollen Zügen aus. Erfahren Sie mehr über diese UNESCO City of Literature und UNESCO-Welterbestätte und finden Sie heraus, was Edinburgh so besonders macht.

Bitte beachten Sie, dass ab dem 1. Oktober 2025 eine Bettensteuer in Höhe von 5 % auf Übernachtungen in Edinburgh ab dem 24. Juli 2026 erhoben wird. Weitere Informationen finden Sie bei ForeverEdinburgh.

Wir wissen, dass zwei der wichtigsten Faktoren bei der Urlaubsplanung die Zeit und die Entfernung sind. Ob Sie nach einem Wochenendtrip suchen, um Ihren Leidenschaften nachzugehen, oder neue Ecken des Landes kennenlernen möchten, wichtig ist, dass Sie viel Zeit für alles haben, was Ihnen Spaß macht, und wenig Zeit mit dem Weg ans Reiseziel verbringen.

Sehen Sie sich diese Vorschläge für tolle Orte an, die sich wunderbar für einen Kurzurlaub in der Woche oder am Wochenende eignen. Alle Ziele sind von Glasgow oder Edinburgh aus in weniger als drei Stunden erreichbar. Planen Sie Ihren Kurzurlaub noch heute!

Edinburgh is de hoofdstad van Schotland. Het is een stad met veel geschiedenis, cultuur, spannende bezienswaardigheden en prachtige architectuur. Ervaar de geplaveide straten, verborgen steegjes, bezoek festivals van wereldklasse en geniet met volle teugen van de fantastische gastronomie. description.value: Visit The Burrell Collection in the heart of Pollok Country Park. This recently refurbished building is home to one of the greatest art collections with thousands of objects spread across 24 galleries and spanning more than 6,000 years of history. You'll find famous artworks from Rodin, Degas and Cézanne, as well as medieval, Chinese and Islamic art.

description.value: GoMA is Scotland's most visited art gallery and the centre for Glasgow's extensive modern and contemporary art collection. ​​​​​​Found in the heart of Glasgow in Royal Exchange Square, with the famous Duke of Wellington Statue guarding out front (usually with a traffic cone atop his head), GoMA is free to enter.

Explore the pathways of Glasgow Botanic Gardens to see their collection of flora and shrubs before visiting Kibble Palace, an exquisite restored Victorian glasshouse. If you're feeling peckish, the tearoom offers a tasty selection of cakes, sandwiches, teas and coffees.

Built in the 1100s, Glasgow Cathedral is one of the oldest buildings in the city and one of the most intact medieval cathedrals on the Scottish mainland.

Visit to see its beautiful Gothic architecture and step down into the historic crypt, which was built in the mid-1200s to house the tomb of St Kentigern.

In Outlander the cathedral’s crypt doubles as L'Hopital Des Anges in Paris, where Claire volunteers to work.
Completed in 1888 and overlooking George Square, Glasgow City Chambers is one of the city’s most prestigious buildings.
Glasgow Science Centre is a great family attraction that presents science and technology in unique and inspiring ways.

description.value: Just south of the River Clyde, explore the works of one of the city's favourite sons, Charles Rennie Mackintosh. The beautiful House for an Art Lover is a stunning Mackintosh design. Although it wasn't actually built in his lifetime, it's a true art nouveau dream fully realised, with the building and furnishings working in complete harmony.

Located in the historic University of Glasgow buildings, the Hunterian Museum has lots of fascinating exhibits and stories for you to uncover, including medicine, zoology and anatomy.

Make sure to visit Mackintosh House too, to see the home that Charles Rennie Mackintosh designed with his wife Margaret Macdonald Mackintosh.

Open: all year

Glasgow's trendy West End is home to Kelvingrove, a free museum packed with over 8,000 intriguing objects and artefacts. The museum houses an extraordinary collection of art, from the Dutch Old Masters and the French Impressionists, to perhaps its most famous piece, Christ of St John of the Cross by Salvador Dali.

The Riverside Museum is a visual feast - the building itself is one of the most striking modern buildings in Scotland. Inside, the museum houses all kinds of exhibits related to transport. Vehicles, bikes, and locomotives dating from different periods can be found. In addition, you can also explore the Clyde-built Tall Ship, which is berthed alongside the museum.
